summary.narrative: Residence Bel-Air Unit 11C, Tower 7 is a 106.2 sqm (2BR+Study) luxury apartment in one of Hong Kong Island's most prestigious seafront developments at 38 Bel-Air Avenue, Pok Fu Lam. The unit is held primarily for Zhang family use during Hong Kong visits, and is partially let through a short-term agent for approximately 6 months per year when unoccupied by the family. The partially-let arrangement generates approximately HKD 132,000 (USD 17,000) annually in net income after management fees. The asset is unencumbered. A formal independent valuation has not been conducted since October 2023 and is overdue.
summary.keyMessage: USD 2.29M unencumbered luxury asset · Partially let ~6 mo/yr · Annual NOI $17,000 · Valuation overdue — last appraisal Oct 2023 · 0.74% cap rate

basicInfo.location: Unit 11C, Tower 7, Residence Bel-Air, 38 Bel-Air Avenue, Pok Fu Lam, Hong Kong
basicInfo.submarket: Pok Fu Lam / Bel-Air — Luxury Residential on HK Island
basicInfo.ownershipSummary: Zhang Family Trust (100%) — direct registered title through BVI holding company

actions.title: Commission Formal Independent Valuation
actions.recommendation: Last independent valuation (Knight Frank HK) was October 2023. A current valuation is required for portfolio reporting accuracy and for the GoWell IPO preparation disclosure package. Engage C H Williams, Colliers, or Savills for a desktop/drive-by valuation — estimated cost HKD 12,000–15,000.

actions.title: Review Partial-Let Strategy vs. Full-Let
actions.recommendation: Current 50% occupancy lets income significantly below full potential. If family usage can be consolidated with Unit 15B Palm Jumeirah or SG West Coast home, the Bel-Air unit could generate HKD 22,000/mo if fully let — improving NOI from $17K to ~$32K USD annually. Recommend family discussion on usage preferences.
actions.decisionNeeded: Family decision on usage frequency — no financial urgency

topRisks.title: HK Residential Market Decline
topRisks.impact: Pok Fu Lam luxury sector has seen 9–14% value decline since 2021 peak due to emigration and reduced mainland Chinese buyer activity.

topRisks.title: Stale Valuation
topRisks.impact: Current portfolio value for this asset may be overstated vs. Oct 2023 appraisal — estimated market decline of 2–5% since last formal valuation.

topRisks.title: Leasehold Expiry (2048)
topRisks.impact: 22-year residual lease is adequate for medium-term hold, but will reduce financing options and require reassessment near the 2040s.

marketSnapshot.submarket: Pok Fu Lam / Bel-Air — Luxury Residential (HK Island)
marketSnapshot.assetVsMarket: Subject: HKD 207/sqm/mo (when let) — slightly below market due to dated fit-out vs. newer comparables
